summary: Recent updates to the WebRTC (the technology behind video calls) functionality in Firefox include the enhancement of its performance metrics and overall reliability. New features like RTCTransportStats, which track the amount of data sent and received during a call, and the improvement of logging practices for better debugging, ensure that developers have the tools they need to monitor and optimize video calls. Furthermore, the implementation of a more robust parser for H.264 video streaming will improve video quality by ensuring that requests and capabilities are better managed. This matters because **_better performance and reliability in video calls can significantly enhance user experience_**, making communications smoother and more efficient.
